narrative.headline: What 2019 Toyota Rav4 owners report most
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints centers on propulsion and engine behavior, with many owners reporting that the vehicle fails to accelerate when the accelerator pedal is pressed, particularly when pulling away from stops. Electrical system glitches and structural issues — including dashboard reflections interfering with mirror visibility — make up a meaningful share of the remaining reports. Complaints across several areas note incidents involving fires or crashes, indicating that some reported symptoms occurred in high-stakes driving situations.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report that the vehicle fails to accelerate when the gas pedal is pressed, with the problem occurring when departing from parked positions or traffic stops while the engine remains running. · 1 fire

issues.sample: VEHICLE WILL NOT ACCELERATE WHEN PUSHING DOWN ON THE GAS PEDAL.  THIS CAN OCCUR WHEN LEAVING A PARKING PLACE OR.  FROM A TRAFFIC LIGHT.  THE ENGINE DOES NOT STOP RUNNING.  IT HAS ONLY HAPPENED THREE TIMES, BUT COULD CAUSE AN ACCIDENT.  THE TOYOTA DEALER COULD NOT DUPLICATE THIS A
issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report a loss of acceleration response when pressing the gas pedal during low-speed departures from parking spots or traffic lights, with the engine continuing to run during the event. · 1 crash

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report visual glitches appearing on the driver-facing center console display, noted even on vehicles with very low mileage shortly after purchase. · 2 fire · 3 crash · 3 injured

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Owners report that reflections from the dashboard obstruct the driver's view in the side rearview mirror. · 1 fire · 2 crash · 1 injured
